pi_items.source: https://imagej.net/images/hela-cells.txt confirmed this is the official sample name in Fiji's File > Open Samples menu
task.json description and verifier.py confirm these filenames

pi_items.verified_value: Fiji built-in sample (HeLa cells fluorescence) - 16-bits/channel composite with red lysosomes, green mitochondria, blue nucleus channels
pi_items.source: https://imagej.net/images/hela-cells.txt - The sample is a fluorescent composite image of HeLa cells with three channels: red (lysosomes), green (mitochondria), blue (nucleus)

pi_items.metadata_value: Separating multiple stains in histology images
pi_items.verified_value: Separating multiple colors in fluorescence microscopy images (NOT histology staining)
pi_items.source: https://imagej.net/plugins/colour-deconvolution and GitHub documentation state that Colour Deconvolution is designed for 'bright field microscopy using histological stains' where 'colours mix subtractively'. Fluorescence microscopy uses additive color mixing (RGB), NOT subtractive. The HeLa Cells sample is documented as a fluorescence microscopy composite image (https://imagej.net/images/hela-cells.txt), not a histologically stained sample.

privileged_info_summary: The HeLa Cells sample is a verified Fiji built-in fluorescence microscopy image containing three 16-bit channels showing lysosomes (red), mitochondria (green), and nucleus (blue). The image is 48-bit RGB format (1.3M file size). However, the task application description is technically incorrect: Colour Deconvolution is designed for histological staining (subtractive color mixing) in bright-field microscopy, not for fluorescence microscopy (additive color mixing). This represents a fundamental mismatch between the analytical tool and the data type.
